author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
I would say it's only a dead end tech if we have something so capable and so available that no one cares about energy period, cause they all have such an abundance that they will never run out. And I have not seen that development to this date, or even any thing that remotely looks like it could even lead to that eventuality LOL! 

love the design updating and upgrading you show over the general Tesla Turbine, (which if i recall was designed to work as it did, so it would not be impeded by friction of static air after getting moving to allow super high speeds since it has very little torque it obtains from the air's reaction with the surface tension of the plates, (if all the air in the chamber is spinning at high speed and the same direction though then this would not even matter, only a fly wheel design is going to benefit from such a system, SO I will post over on discord an idea this and the basic design gave me, for an upgrade from even these changes XD

author: @rogerbraintree9552
text:
I get confused with these efficiency ratings.  If one sprayed a jet of steam at the Pelton wheel, one would surely have potentially a highly efficient external combustion engine.  For about 130 degrees C of heat one can get a couple of bars or 28psi worth of pressure.  The Carnot efficiency reduces overall efficiency by 50% or so.  But with that wood burning stove you made, by wrapping refrigerator pipe around it in a coil and perhaps having some of the coil inside the chimney, and then wrapping rockwool around it to make really good heat insulation, then all of that heat would be concentrated into the pipe.

Another idea is that if it is harder to direct a jet of steam at a Pelton wheel owing to its lower mass, if the steam pipe was fed into a tank of water, this would increase the water pressure which could in turn be used with the water wheel you just made.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I was reading some papers on efficiency and how it is calculated and what is and isn't taken into account - the conclusion was - it's all a bit confusing lol - on a serious note efficiency is one of those concepts that by itself means nothing and one of those concepts that we think means something - like freedom - it means different things to different people so when we say it we think we have communicated something but it hasn't as the speaker and the listener mean different things by it - anyway enough philosophy - like the practical ideas mate - but then I like practical ideas lol

author: @totherarf
text:
A couple of thoughts ocurred to me ..... nothing unusual about that, but thes may be relevant!

On your original water motor you have the inlet and the exhaust at opposing angles. This would force the water to change direction .... not good for efficiency! Did you make it like that for a reason? ...... If you changed your inlet position / angle would it make a difference?
You say making a Tesla Turbine is a pain (I agree) because of the high speed of rotation, tollerances and balance. Would this still be true if you used water rather than air? It would seem that the increased viscosity between air and water would both increase the rotational power given and at the same time reduce the rate of rotation due to interferance with the water and the disc and the outer container. 

The most important question is ..... How do you keep on coming up with new old tech that is so fascinating?
Whatever .... Please don't stop!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @pikotech1
text:
Another interesting little device Rob. You never fail to impress me with your ability to find potential even with "redundant" technologies.

Quick question for you if you don't mind? How quickly do you usually ship your conductive ink? I placed and order this morning, and just trying to get and idea of when I will be able to find time to take a look at my ideas on modification to the rainwater generator. Copper tape strips, conductive ink, polyurethane with tungsten electrodes (copper electrodes would oxidise) is the plan. Of course I'll give you and update when / if I get there!

I've had a chemist friend join me in my experimentation with this, and we have a sneaky feeling the rain drops can be recycled (re-gain electrons) with a drop of 5cm or so onto another generator panel. Exciting indeed! 

Cheers mate. Always a big fan of your work!

EDIT: Just got an email with a tracking number from Royal Mail for the ink. Anyone else reading this, Rob has a pretty good shop going on there! 😉

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it went out yesterday mate - we tend to ship same day  - I am excited o see your update mate - I like the plan you have there
